channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Lawmakers push to expand agencies' power to cope with drones
item.description: Lawmakers continue to discuss legislation that would expand the authority of local police agencies to deal with drones.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Americans are exhausted by political news. TV ratings and a new AP-NORC poll show they're tuning out
item.description: A lot of Americans, after an intense presidential election campaign, are looking for a break in political news.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder And Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance is the likely 2028 frontrunner, but these Republicans may also run for president
item.description: Vice president-elect JD Vance is the clear frontrunner for the 2028 GOP presidential nomination at this extremely early point, but here's a look at other Republicans who may mull a White House run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oregon AG creates sanctuary ‘toolkit’ ahead of likely Trump-Homan illegal immigration crackdown
item.description: Oregon Attorney General Ellen Rosenblum published a "sanctuary promise community toolkit" to help inform Oregonians about state laws that protect them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The bald eagle is officially America’s national bird, thanks to a Minnesotan
item.description: After learning the U.S. doesn’t officially recognize the bald eagle as its national bird, a Minnesota man swooped in and wrote a bill for Congress. This week, Biden signed it into law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trial of man accused in Trump assassination attempt in Florida pushed back to September
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — A man accused of attempting to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump in South Florida won't be tried until September 2025, a federal judge ruled this week. Ryan Routh's trial will begin Sept. 8 instead of the previously scheduled Feb. 10, 2025 start date, U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The top five absurd tips from liberal pundits for surviving holidays with Trump-voting family
item.description: Liberal outlets offered advice on "Trump-proofing" Christmas, from canceling holidays altogether to awkward scripts for family talks, sparking debate over festive unity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fate of Trump's Cabinet picks unclear as Republicans prepare to take power in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The fate of President-elect Donald Trump’s Cabinet is still unclear after Republican senators spent much of December carefully dodging questions about Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden will decide on US Steel acquisition after influential panel fails to reach consensus
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A powerful government panel on Monday failed to reach consensus on the possible national security risks of a nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase U.S. Steel, leaving the decision to President Joe Biden, who opposes the deal.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARC LEVY and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs defense bill despite objections to ban on transgender health care for military children
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday signed into law a defense bill that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion despite his objections to language stripping coverage
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden’s death row commutations draws chorus of mixed reactions
item.description: A chorus of supporters and critics have emerged following the commutation of nearly every federal death row inmate by President Biden, whose decision came at a time when he’s trying to frame a so-far troubled legacy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Read: House Ethics report on Matt Gaetz allegations
item.description: Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) had moved earlier in the day to block the release of the report through a lawsuit, arguing that the committee was acting beyond its authority.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-US Rep. Gaetz paid women for sex while in Congress: Ethics report
item.description: Ex-U.S. Rep. Matt Gaetz paid tens of thousands to women for sex and drugs, according to a draft of a House ethics report.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 federal death row prisoners
item.description: Opponents of the death penalty had urged President Joe Biden to take this step, given the number of executions that took place during President-elect Donald Trump's first term.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump suggests Panama Canal be returned to US control
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday again took on the matter of the Panama Canal, suggesting to a conference of his supporters that it be returned to U.S. control in a remarkable suggestion to push a sovereign country to cede territory.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Government handling of drones a 'catastrophe': Elizondo
item.description: The FAA temporarily banned drone flights in areas of New Jersey and New York, where critical infrastructure is located.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: NJ rep. says it's alarming Feds can't bring down one drone
item.description: Chris Smith is disappointed by what he sees as a lack of transparency by the Biden administration over the New Jersey drone flap.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats condemn ethical lapses by Thomas, Supreme Court justices
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee condemned the Supreme Court for an “ethical crisis of its own making” in a new report released Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: What is next for Florida's insurance market as CFO Patronis steps down
item.description: He is the state's top insurance official, but Florida's Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis is stepping down in a few months to run for Congress. Governor Ron DeSantis will appoint a temporary replacement to the CFO position, as Florida residents continue to face a fragile insurance market.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada election leaders find nearly 200 cases of double votes from November
item.description: The Nevada Secretary of State’s Office is investigating 180 double votes from the 2024 general election, a report released Friday indicated.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s reelection victory pushes some LGTBQ Minneapolis residents to safety training
item.description: For some marginalized communities the second Trump term is fraught with fears over personal safety. In Minneapolis, one organization is helping with de-escalation services and gun training.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News
